purpose: Prove the ext_stress synthetic hostcall path drives the real extension hostcall reactor queues before future NUMA or lane-placement decisions use reactor metrics.

harness_changes.synthetic_hostcall: agent_start handler calls pi.events("list_flags", {}), which resolves to a fast-lane hostcall and is submitted through the reactor mesh when enabled.

usage.command: rch exec -- cargo run --example ext_stress -- --synthetic-hostcall-extensions 4 --synthetic-hostcall-root /data/tmp/pi_agent_rust_cargo/rusticgorge-bd-2zcs5-24/direct-rch/synthetic --event agent_start --reactor-enabled=true --reactor-shards 4 --reactor-lane-capacity 128 --reactor-drain-budget 128 --duration-secs 2 --warmup-secs 0 --rss-interval-secs 1 --events-per-sec 2 --dispatch-timeout-ms 2000 --require-reactor-activity=true --report-path /data/tmp/pi_agent_rust_cargo/rusticgorge-bd-2zcs5-24/direct-rch/ext-stress-reactor-queue-coverage.json --events-path /data/tmp/pi_agent_rust_cargo/rusticgorge-bd-2zcs5-24/direct-rch/ext-stress-events.jsonl --run-id bd-2zcs5-24-reactor-proof --correlation-id bd-2zcs5-24-reactor-proof
notes: Use a synthetic_hostcall_root outside docs/evidence so generated JS extension sources do not become repository artifacts.
The run should exit nonzero when --require-reactor-activity=true and either enqueue or dispatch counters remain zero.
The report keeps normal ext_stress RSS, latency, event-count, and comparison fields intact.

stdout: Reactor activity: enabled=true, enqueued_total=16, dispatched_total=16, active_shards=4, ok=true
direct_rch_gate_proof.artifact_note: The direct RCH command wrote the JSON report on the remote worker under /data/tmp; the stdout gate result is the direct remote proof retained here.
